evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: self-serve insight products that are fast, accurate, and elastic at a petabyte-scale
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD balances speed/scale language ("fast", "elastic at a petabyte-scale", "rapidly evolving, hyper-growth environments") with rigour signals ("stewards of data quality", "ensuring that metrics are consistently reported"), landing on parity.
evidence.domain_risk: self-serve insight products that are fast, accurate, and elastic at a petabyte-scale
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Mobile gaming analytics affects product and player-behavior decisions but has no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance exposure, and the JD contains no such language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: We work with cutting-edge tech (BigQuery, dbt, Looker)
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools appear directly in responsibilities and the team already runs a full data product lifecycle with dashboards and forecasting, but the framing ("often specialize in some part of the data stack") suggests a still-forming team rather than a large mature org.
evidence.jd_authorship: designing new tracking plans, developing data models, ELT pipelines, and self-serve insight products like Looker explores and dashboards
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools, deliverables (tracking plans, Looker explores, BQML forecasting), and scale ("petabyte-scale"), giving a concrete picture of the day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: We empathize with our game product teams and departments to make their daily work more enjoyable and their products more successful
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves internal engineering/product/analyst consumers of self-serve data products rather than an external commercial or finance audience.
evidence.autonomy_level: We often specialize in some part of the data stack and lifecycle but always aim to contribute across the full spectrum
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role mixes broad technical ownership across the data lifecycle with a collaborative, in-service posture toward product teams, landing as mixed rather than purely strategic or purely execution.
evidence.ai_role: applying tools like BQML to predict player behavior
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build/apply ML forecasting (BQML) as part of the data infrastructure, making the candidate's output feed downstream predictive systems rather than using AI coding tools themselves.
evidence.testing_framing: We are stewards of data quality, ensuring that metrics are consistently reported across the catalog of data products
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality stewardship is framed as an owned responsibility with an accountable verb ("stewards of"), not merely a tool in a skills list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is framed entirely in delivery/capability terms (self-serve products, forecasting, collaboration) with no risk, compliance, or trust register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: We work across the full data product lifecycle: designing new tracking plans, developing data models, ELT pipelines
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both designs new tracking plans (greenfield) and maintains/extends an existing catalog of data products (scale), making it a mix.
